developer.name: James Carmichael
developer.title: Phantom Thief of Web Development
developer.bio: A passionate web developer who discovered their calling in high school. Through three years of learning, failing, and growing, I've built everything from simple HTML pages to full-stack experiences. The web is my canvas, code is my paint.

projects.title: HTML Foundations Blog
projects.description: My very first web project — a personal blog built from scratch using pure HTML and CSS. Learned the fundamentals of web structure and visual design.
projects.reflection: This was where it all began. I remember staring at a blank HTML file wondering what to type. By the end, I had something I was genuinely proud of.

projects.title: Responsive Photography Portfolio
projects.description: A responsive photography showcase site built with CSS Grid and Flexbox. My first serious attempt at mobile-first design.
projects.reflection: Learning that websites needed to work on phones changed everything for me. Responsive design felt like a superpower.

projects.title: JavaScript Calculator
projects.description: An interactive calculator app — my introduction to JavaScript programming and DOM manipulation.
projects.reflection: The moment my calculator worked for the first time, I felt like a real programmer. It was magical.

projects.title: Weather Dashboard App
projects.description: A real-time weather application using the OpenWeather API. My first experience with async JavaScript and API integration.
projects.reflection: Working with external APIs for the first time felt like unlocking a cheat code. Suddenly my apps could talk to the real world.

projects.title: Vue.js Task Manager
projects.description: A full-featured task management app built with Vue.js — complete with filtering, categories, and local storage persistence.
projects.reflection: Vue.js completely changed how I thought about building interfaces. Reactive data felt like magic at first.

projects.title: E-Commerce Mockup
projects.description: A complete e-commerce UI mockup with product pages, cart functionality, and checkout flow.
projects.reflection: This project taught me the importance of UX thinking. Good design isn't just about looks — it's about how things work.

projects.title: This Portfolio
projects.description: The one you're looking at right now. A Persona 5-inspired interactive portfolio experience built as my senior capstone project.
projects.reflection: Building this felt like everything I'd learned coming together. Every challenge from the past three years prepared me for this moment.

projects.title: Music Visualizer
projects.description: An interactive audio visualizer using the Web Audio API with custom canvas animations.
projects.reflection: Combining my love of music and programming felt deeply personal. This project represents who I am beyond just code.

reflection.overall: Three years ago, I opened a text editor for the first time and typed my first HTML tag. I had no idea that simple moment would reshape who I am. Web design taught me that creativity and logic aren't opposites — they're partners. Every bug I fixed made me more patient. Every design I iterated made me more perceptive. Every project I shipped made me more confident.
reflection.challenges: The hardest moments came from imposter syndrome. Looking at professional websites and thinking 'I'll never be that good.' But challenges became teachers. Every 'why isn't this working' became a lesson. Every broken layout became a puzzle worth solving.
reflection.favorites: My favorite memory is staying up until 3am to finish a project, not because I had to, but because I genuinely couldn't stop. That's when I knew web design wasn't just a class — it was a calling.
reflection.transformation: Web design changed how I see everything. Every website I visit, I notice the typography, the spacing, the hover interactions. It's like getting a new pair of eyes. I can't unsee design anymore, and I wouldn't want to.

futurePlans.college: Planning to study Computer Science or Interactive Media Design at a 4-year university.
futurePlans.career: Aspiring to become a UI/UX engineer — someone who bridges the gap between beautiful design and functional code.
goals: Intern at a tech company during freshman year
Build and launch a real product by junior year
Eventually start my own design studio
futurePlans.message: Web design showed me that the internet is a creative medium as expressive as any art form. I want to spend my life making it more beautiful, more accessible, and more human.
